mainEntity.name: How much does a BMW 325d engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned 325d engine costs £3,200–£6,000 supply only depending on generation. E90 M57/N57 straight-sixes start around £3,500; F30 N47 four-cylinders start around £3,200; G20 B47 units start around £3,400. Fitting adds £650–£1,500. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

It depends entirely on which of the four engine generations your 325d has. The M57 (E90 pre-LCI) is a robust iron-block straight-six; the N57 (E90 LCI) is more refined but has rear-chain considerations; the N47 (F30 pre-LCI) is the well-documented rear-chain risk generation; the B47 (F30 LCI/G20) has the chain relocated to the front and is meaningfully improved. Check the Era Map above for your specific car.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the BMW 325d?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain wear on N47 and N57 engines, swirl flap failure on the M57, and EGR cooler coolant leaks affecting N57 and N47 engines (subject to a BMW recall). The 325d badge spans multiple engine families, so the specific issues depend entirely on which generation you have.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a BMW 325d engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Depends on vehicle value and which generation. Against a well-kept £10,000 325d, a £4,500 recon is 45% — worth it. Against a tired £4,000 example, the same recon exceeds our 70% BMW threshold — get a scrap valuation first.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my BMW 325d engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: Listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. As a rule of thumb: E90 pre-LCI (2006–2008) is M57N2; E90 LCI (2008–2011) is N57D30U0; F30 pre-LCI (2011–2015) is N47D20D; F30 LCI/G20 (2015–present) is B47D20. The VIN is the only sure way — tell us your registration and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the N47 timing chain, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. A preventative chain replacement (£650–£1,800) is far cheaper than a catastrophic chain snap (£3,200–£5,200 engine replacement). At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately. For the N57, the same principle applies — less common but still a known pattern.

eraMap.title: The 325d THROUGH TIME — Era Map

rows.eraNote: The original 325d — iron-block M57. Robust, but swirl flaps and thermostats are known weak points.
Alloy-block N57. More refined and better on fuel; rear timing chain, EGR and DPF need vigilance.

rows.reliability: ★★★☆☆ 1,450 JLR-wide [BMW-VERIFIED — inherited from Model Page & F30 Generation Page]
rows.eraNote: The four-cylinder 325d — rear timing chain risk applies.
Chain relocated to the front — a genuinely different risk profile.

eraMap.sourceNote: This table is the single source every other section on this page matches — Sections 2, 4 and 6 reference it rather than restating conflicting figures.

replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the current G20/B47 generation, the most common quote request today. Older generations: see the Era Map above for E90/M57/N57 and F30/N47-specific pricing, which runs lower.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a 325d engine typically takes 10–16 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer).

problems.title: N47 Rear Timing Chain Failure (F30 pre-LCI)
problems.symptoms: Cold-start rattle lasting 1–30 seconds, metallic ticking, engine warning light.

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattle, book an inspection today.

problems.recommendation: This applies to the four-cylinder N47-powered F30 325d (pre-LCI). At the first sign of cold-start rattle, get the chain inspected. A £650–£1,800 preventative repair is dramatically cheaper than the £3,200+ replacement that follows.

failureLink.label: Read the full timing chain failure guide

problems.title: N57 Timing Chain & EGR Cooler (E90 LCI)
problems.symptoms: Rattling noise on cold start (timing chain), coolant loss with white smoke (EGR cooler), engine warning light.

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: if you hear rattle or see coolant loss, book an inspection today.

problems.recommendation: The N57 has a rear-mounted timing chain design similar to the N47 — it's less prone to failure than the four-cylinder but still a known pattern on neglected oil changes. The EGR cooler recall (2018–2019) affected many N57-equipped vehicles. Verify recall completion before buying an LCI E90 325d.

failureLink.label: Read the full timing chain failure guide

problems.title: Swirl Flap Failure (M57 E90 pre-LCI)
problems.symptoms: Loss of power, rough idle, engine warning light, occasional rattling from the intake.
problems.replacementCost: £850–£1,200 (intake manifold, or engine repair if debris entered a cylinder).
problems.recommendation: The M57 is a robust engine, but swirl flaps are its Achilles heel. Preventive blanking is cheap and effective. This applies to E90 pre-LCI 325d with the M57 engine only — the N57 does not suffer from swirl flap issues.

failureLink.label: Read the full swirl flap failure guide

Preventative — less common than N47 but still a pattern

buyingChecks.title: Buying This 325d? Check These First
buyIf: * E90 pre-LCI: Documented swirl flap blanking or replacement
* E90 LCI: Confirmed EGR recall completion and timing chain inspection
* Any generation: Full service history with sub-15,000km oil intervals
avoidIf: * E90 pre-LCI: No proof of swirl flap work
* E90 LCI: Cold-start rattle or EGR cooler failure with no documented repair
* F30 pre-LCI: Cold-start rattle and no chain history
* Any generation: Poor service history — these engines are unforgiving of neglect

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The 325d is one of the most confusing badges in BMW's modern lineup — it started as a 3.0-litre straight-six, then became a 2.0-litre four-cylinder. The M57 and N57 straight-sixes are the purist's choice; the N47 and B47 four-cylinders are the modern economy option. Buy the engine that matches your priorities — but buy on documented history, not on faith, regardless of which generation you choose.

cta.label: → Compare 325d engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used BMW 325d (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattle (N47/N57 timing chain warning; M57 swirl flap warning)
* Service History — including which engine era (M57/N57/N47/B47) and confirmed oil-interval discipline
* Smoke Check — White smoke on startup can indicate EGR coolant leak (N57/N47)
* Swirl Flap Check (M57) — Have swirl flaps been blanked or replaced?
* DPF Regeneration — Check history; short journeys accelerate wear on all diesel eras
* Oil Consumption — Does it use more than 1L per 1,000 miles?
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
